summary: Reviewed the host-controller-tweaks spec and changed documentation through failure-path/workflow risks. The main workflow risk is an internal spec mismatch around how first-game-only house rules are passed into the proposed briefing component; the pack does not include implementation code, so live UI behavior and viewport claims remain verification items rather than confirmed defects.
appears_sound: The spec preserves server-side call-delay enforcement and only removes the host-facing hint, so the pacing guard remains on `game_states.call_delay_seconds` rather than moving into client UI (`tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:35`).

The proposed briefing trigger deliberately re-shows at zero calls after Undo Last Call, which gives the host a recoverable way to return to the pre-game readout without special state (`tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:131`).
Display/player scope is explicitly excluded, and the display house-rules refactor is described as data-source-only with no visual change intended (`tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:7`).

findings.description: Failure sequence: game 2 opens at zero calls, `GameControl` knows `isFirstGameOfSession`, but the proposed `PreGameBriefing` props omit that flag; implementation can easily either show HOUSE RULES on every game or suppress them everywhere. That breaks the host workflow requirement that rules appear once per session while prize briefings appear before every game.
findings.evidence: The spec requires first-game-only rules via `isFirstGameOfSession` but later defines the new component props as only `game` and `currentSnowballPot` in the file-by-file impact table (`tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:151`, `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:177`).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: GameControl could render the house-rules block outside the briefing component or pass an undocumented prop despite the impact table.
findings.what_would_confirm: Update the spec/component contract to include `showHouseRules` or state exactly where the conditional rules block is rendered, then verify game 1 and game 2 zero-call screens.

findings.description: Failure sequence: the host opens game 1 on an iPhone 14 Pro, the larger briefing with rules and prize ladder renders above the controls, and the controls still require scrolling despite the goal saying no scrolling. The spacing table verifies the in-play one-number state, but the pre-game-with-rules state is taller and is the exact moment when the host needs Next Number, Take Break, and Check Claim visible.
findings.evidence: The verification requires game 1 briefing plus controls without scrolling, but the spacing target table is scoped to a game with at least one number called and therefore excludes the largest pre-game rules layout (`tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:136`, `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:188`).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The briefing component may be compact enough in the actual implementation, or the host may tolerate scrolling only before the first call.
findings.what_would_confirm: Capture the implemented game 1 zero-call screen at 390x664 and verify the bottom edge of Check Claim is inside the viewport.

empty_categories: No retry/idempotency defects found in the proposed UI-only changes; number calling remains server-side per the spec.
No cancellation or cleanup issues found because the pack proposes no new async mutation flow or background process.
No partial-completion database risks found in this spec; it explicitly avoids server-action, RLS, and data-model changes.
